title: BayBridge Auto Injury firm profile
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Firm Profile

## Role in AutoCase Network

General Bay Area auto-injury firm

## Coverage

Alameda, San Francisco, San Mateo, Santa Clara, Contra Costa

## Best-fit matters

- Bay Area rear-end collisions with injury
- simple two-car crashes with mostly clear liability
- Bay Area Uber/Lyft passenger injuries with moderate injury
- neck/back pain cases where caller has or plans medical treatment

## Strong-fit subtypes

rear_end_clear_liability, simple_two_car, bay_area_rideshare

## Network referral logic

When a case is not a strong fit for BayBridge Auto Injury, use the following partner referrals:

- commercial vehicle plus ER/hospital/severe injury -> Summit Commercial Auto
- multi-car disputed fault or everyone blaming each other -> Pacific Complex Collision
- hit-and-run or uninsured/underinsured complexity -> Pacific Complex Collision
- Southern California accident or Spanish-speaking SoCal caller -> Vista Auto Justice
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Current demo capacity

- Consult slots today: 3
- Urgent slots today: 1
- Max new reviews per day: 6

## Attorneys

- Sarah Kim: rear-end and simple auto injury; slots today: 3:00 PM, 4:30 PM
- David Lee: rideshare and moderate injury; slots today: 2:00 PM

## Example strong-fit caller

"I was rear-ended in Oakland. My neck hurts and the other driver's insurance keeps calling."

## Example referral caller

"A FedEx van hit me and I went to the ER."

title: BayBridge Auto Injury auto intake checklist
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Auto Accident Intake Checklist

## First-pass intake goals

Collect enough facts to determine:
1. Whether there is an injury.
2. Whether the accident is inside coverage.
3. Which auto subtype applies.
4. Whether the case fits BayBridge Auto Injury or should be referred to another AutoCase Network firm.
5. Whether the caller needs attorney review, referral, resource response, or more information.

## Minimum required fields

- Caller full name
- Phone number
- Email, if available
- Preferred language
- Relationship to injured person
- Accident date
- Accident location: city, county, state, freeway/intersection if available
- Accident subtype
- Injury description
- Medical treatment: none, planned, urgent care, ER, hospital, surgery
- Minor involved: yes/no/unknown
- Insurance contact: yes/no/unknown
- Police report: yes/no/unknown
- Photos/video/witnesses: yes/no/unknown

## Auto subtype prompts

### Rear-end / clear-liability collision
Ask:
- Were you stopped or moving?
- Were you hit from behind?
- Did police make a report?
- Are you experiencing neck, back, or head pain?
- Did insurance contact you?

### Rideshare accident
Ask:
- Was Uber or Lyft involved?
- Were you a passenger, driver, pedestrian, or another driver?
- Was the app on or was a ride in progress?
- Which insurance companies have contacted you?

### Commercial vehicle accident
Ask:
- Was the other vehicle a company car, delivery van, work truck, or fleet vehicle?
- Do you know the company name?
- Did you go to the ER or hospital?
- Has a company insurer contacted you?

### Multi-car / disputed liability
Ask:
- How many vehicles were involved?
- Is anyone blaming you?
- Was there a police report?
- Are multiple insurance companies involved?

### Hit-and-run / uninsured
Ask:
- Did the other driver leave the scene?
- Do you know if they had insurance?
- Did you file a police report?
- Do you know whether you have uninsured motorist coverage?

### Property damage only
Ask:
- Was anyone injured?
- Are you seeking only vehicle repair or property damage reimbursement?

## BayBridge Auto Injury fit note

Best-fit cases for this firm:
- Bay Area rear-end collisions with injury
- simple two-car crashes with mostly clear liability
- Bay Area Uber/Lyft passenger injuries with moderate injury
- neck/back pain cases where caller has or plans medical treatment

## Referral note

If the case is a better fit for another network firm, do not share client information until consent is granted.

title: BayBridge Auto Injury case acceptance and referral criteria
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Case Acceptance and Referral Criteria

## Accept criteria

BayBridge Auto Injury should generally accept or request attorney review when:

- Bay Area rear-end collisions with injury
- simple two-car crashes with mostly clear liability
- Bay Area Uber/Lyft passenger injuries with moderate injury
- neck/back pain cases where caller has or plans medical treatment

Additional required signals:
- Injury is mentioned.
- The caller is in coverage or the case has statewide coverage fit.
- The facts do not require a better-matched specialist firm.
- Attorney capacity is available or urgent review is justified.

## Priority triggers

Escalate priority when any of the following are present:
- Minor or child involved.
- ER, hospital, surgery, fracture, brain/spine injury, or severe ongoing symptoms.
- Commercial vehicle or employer-owned vehicle.
- Insurance company is pressuring the caller.
- Multiple parties are blaming each other.
- Hit-and-run or uninsured/underinsured driver.
- Caller asks "Do I have a case?" or requests legal evaluation.

## Decline/resource triggers

Use a decline/resource path when:
- No one was injured.
- Property damage only.
- Caller only wants repair reimbursement.
- Accident occurred outside service area and no network firm applies.
- Caller refuses consent for referral and no other workflow is available.

## Refer-out criteria

- commercial vehicle plus ER/hospital/severe injury -> Summit Commercial Auto
- multi-car disputed fault or everyone blaming each other -> Pacific Complex Collision
- hit-and-run or uninsured/underinsured complexity -> Pacific Complex Collision
- Southern California accident or Spanish-speaking SoCal caller -> Vista Auto Justice
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Attorney review threshold

Send to attorney review when:
- Case is a possible fit but missing facts prevent acceptance.
- Minor involved and injury is possible.
- Caller asks for case-strength evaluation.
- High-priority facts are present but the matching firm should confirm.
- Capacity is limited and attorney must approve intake.

## Retrieval examples

- Query: "I was rear-ended in Oakland. My neck hurts and the other driver's insurance keeps calling." -> retrieve this firm's acceptance criteria.
- Query: "A FedEx van hit me and I went to the ER." -> retrieve referral criteria and partner match.

title: BayBridge Auto Injury intake call script
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Intake Call Script

## Opening

"I'm sorry that happened. I can collect the key details for attorney review. I am not a lawyer and cannot determine whether you have a case, but I can help get the right information to the right team."

## Core questions

1. "Where and when did the accident happen?"
2. "Was anyone injured or treated by urgent care, the ER, or a doctor?"
3. "Was a child or minor involved?"
4. "Was a commercial vehicle, Uber/Lyft, multiple cars, hit-and-run, or uninsured driver involved?"
5. "Has any insurance company contacted you?"
6. "What is the best phone number for the firm to follow up?"

## Follow-up questions based on retrieved context

### If commercial vehicle
"Do you know the company name on the vehicle?"

### If ER/hospital
"Are you still receiving medical treatment?"

### If minor involved
"Was the child injured or evaluated by a medical provider?"

### If insurance pressure
"Have they asked for a recorded statement or for you to sign anything?"

### If referral likely
"Based on what you shared, this may be a better fit for a trusted partner firm. Your information is confidential, and we will not share it without your permission."

## Closing for attorney review

"Thank you. I will prepare this for attorney review and mark the missing details so the team can follow up."

## Closing for referral consent

"Do you consent to us sharing your case summary and contact information with the recommended partner firm so they can review whether they may be able to help?"

title: BayBridge Auto Injury referral consent policy
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Referral Consent and Confidentiality Policy

## Confidential information

The following information must be treated as confidential and should not be shared with a partner firm until client consent is granted:

- Caller name
- Phone number
- Email
- Accident location/date
- Injury description
- Medical treatment details
- Insurance details
- Transcript excerpts
- Photos, documents, police report information, or witness details

## Consent prompt

"Based on what you shared, this matter may be a better fit for one of our trusted partner firms, [Receiving Firm]. Your information is confidential, and we will not share your case summary or contact information without your permission. Do you consent to us sharing your intake details with [Receiving Firm] so they can review whether they may be able to help?"

## Consent outcomes

### If consent is granted
- Mark consent status: granted.
- Unlock referral packet.
- Send packet to receiving firm.
- Notify client that packet has been sent.
- Create referral tracking record.

### If consent is denied
- Mark consent status: denied.
- Keep packet locked.
- Do not share externally.
- Offer a general resource response or ask whether the client wants more information.

### If client wants more information
- Provide partner firm name, coverage, why it may be a fit, and what information would be shared.
- Ask again for consent before sharing.

## Audit fields

- Consent status
- Timestamp
- Receiving firm
- Information categories shared
- User approving referral
- Packet sent status

title: BayBridge Auto Injury decline and resource templates
text:
# BayBridge Auto Injury — Resource and Decline Response Templates

## Property damage only

"Based on what you shared, this appears to involve vehicle or property damage without an injury. This may not require a personal-injury attorney. We can provide general resources for handling a vehicle damage claim, but we will not use attorney review capacity for this intake."

## Outside coverage but network fit

"This may be outside our direct coverage, but it may be a fit for a trusted partner firm in our network. Your information is confidential and will not be shared unless you give permission."

## Missing injury information

"To route this correctly, I need to know whether anyone was injured or received medical treatment."

## Missing contact information

"What is the best phone number and email for the attorney team or referral partner to follow up if you choose to proceed?"

title: Summit Commercial Auto firm profile
text:
# Summit Commercial Auto — Firm Profile

## Role in AutoCase Network

Commercial vehicle and severe-injury auto specialist

## Coverage

California statewide

## Best-fit matters

- delivery van, company vehicle, work truck, fleet vehicle accidents
- ER, hospitalization, surgery, fracture, brain/spine injury, or death
- commercial insurer or employer representative contacted caller
- high-severity auto cases even if not fully investigated yet

## Strong-fit subtypes

commercial_vehicle, commercial_vehicle_severe, catastrophic_auto, wrongful_death_auto

## Network referral logic

When a case is not a strong fit for Summit Commercial Auto, use the following partner referrals:

- simple Bay Area rear-end with moderate injury -> BayBridge Auto Injury
- multi-car dispute without commercial or severe injury -> Pacific Complex Collision
- hit-and-run or uninsured issue without commercial defendant -> Pacific Complex Collision
- Southern California standard/moderate auto case -> Vista Auto Justice
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Current demo capacity

- Consult slots today: 1
- Urgent slots today: 1
- Max new reviews per day: 3

## Attorneys

- Maya Patel: commercial vehicle and severe injury; slots today: 5:00 PM urgent
- Jordan Price: catastrophic injury review; slots today: none

## Example strong-fit caller

"A FedEx delivery van hit me in San Jose and I went to the ER."

## Example referral caller

"I was rear-ended in Oakland and my neck hurts."

title: Pacific Complex Collision firm profile
text:
# Pacific Complex Collision — Firm Profile

## Role in AutoCase Network

Complex liability and insurance-dispute auto firm

## Coverage

California statewide, Bay Area, Sacramento

## Best-fit matters

- multi-car accidents, pileups, chain reactions
- unclear fault or caller being blamed
- hit-and-run, uninsured/underinsured driver issues
- rideshare coverage disputes or multiple insurance carriers

## Strong-fit subtypes

multi_car_disputed, hit_and_run_uninsured, coverage_dispute, disputed_liability

## Network referral logic

When a case is not a strong fit for Pacific Complex Collision, use the following partner referrals:

- simple clear-liability Bay Area rear-end -> BayBridge Auto Injury
- commercial vehicle with severe injury -> Summit Commercial Auto
- Southern California standard auto case -> Vista Auto Justice
- Spanish-speaking Southern California caller -> Vista Auto Justice
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Current demo capacity

- Consult slots today: 2
- Urgent slots today: 0
- Max new reviews per day: 5

## Attorneys

- Elena Torres: hit-and-run and uninsured motorist; slots today: 1:30 PM
- Nathan Brooks: multi-car liability disputes; slots today: 4:00 PM

## Example strong-fit caller

"Three cars were involved and everyone is blaming each other. I am injured and do not know whose insurance to call."

## Example referral caller

"A delivery truck hit me and I was hospitalized."

- Ask again for consent before sharing.

## Audit fields

- Consent status
- Timestamp
- Receiving firm
- Information categories shared
- User approving referral
- Packet sent status

title: Vista Auto Justice firm profile
text:
# Vista Auto Justice — Firm Profile

## Role in AutoCase Network

Southern California auto-injury firm with bilingual intake

## Coverage

Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside, San Bernardino, San Diego

## Best-fit matters

- Southern California rear-end or simple auto accidents with injury
- Spanish-speaking callers or bilingual intake needed
- Southern California Uber/Lyft injuries
- moderate commercial-vehicle accidents that are not catastrophic

## Strong-fit subtypes

southern_california_auto, southern_california_rideshare, spanish_speaking_auto

## Network referral logic

When a case is not a strong fit for Vista Auto Justice, use the following partner referrals:

- severe commercial or catastrophic injury -> Summit Commercial Auto
- complex uninsured, hit-and-run, or disputed liability -> Pacific Complex Collision
- Bay Area clear-liability case -> BayBridge Auto Injury
- Bay Area disputed liability -> Pacific Complex Collision
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Current demo capacity

- Consult slots today: 4
- Urgent slots today: 1
- Max new reviews per day: 8

## Attorneys

- Camila Rivera: Spanish-speaking auto injury intake; slots today: 12:30 PM, 3:30 PM
- Anthony Nguyen: SoCal rideshare and moderate injury; slots today: 2:30 PM, 5:30 PM

## Example strong-fit caller

"Me chocaron en Los Angeles. Tengo dolor en la espalda. Necesito hablar con alguien en español."

## Example referral caller

"A UPS truck hit me and I had surgery."

title: Vista Auto Justice auto intake checklist
text:
# Vista Auto Justice — Auto Accident Intake Checklist

## First-pass intake goals

Collect enough facts to determine:
1. Whether there is an injury.
2. Whether the accident is inside coverage.
3. Which auto subtype applies.
4. Whether the case fits Vista Auto Justice or should be referred to another AutoCase Network firm.
5. Whether the caller needs attorney review, referral, resource response, or more information.

## Minimum required fields

- Caller full name
- Phone number
- Email, if available
- Preferred language
- Relationship to injured person
- Accident date
- Accident location: city, county, state, freeway/intersection if available
- Accident subtype
- Injury description
- Medical treatment: none, planned, urgent care, ER, hospital, surgery
- Minor involved: yes/no/unknown
- Insurance contact: yes/no/unknown
- Police report: yes/no/unknown
- Photos/video/witnesses: yes/no/unknown

## Auto subtype prompts

### Rear-end / clear-liability collision
Ask:
- Were you stopped or moving?
- Were you hit from behind?
- Did police make a report?
- Are you experiencing neck, back, or head pain?
- Did insurance contact you?

### Rideshare accident
Ask:
- Was Uber or Lyft involved?
- Were you a passenger, driver, pedestrian, or another driver?
- Was the app on or was a ride in progress?
- Which insurance companies have contacted you?

### Commercial vehicle accident
Ask:
- Was the other vehicle a company car, delivery van, work truck, or fleet vehicle?
- Do you know the company name?
- Did you go to the ER or hospital?
- Has a company insurer contacted you?

### Multi-car / disputed liability
Ask:
- How many vehicles were involved?
- Is anyone blaming you?
- Was there a police report?
- Are multiple insurance companies involved?

### Hit-and-run / uninsured
Ask:
- Did the other driver leave the scene?
- Do you know if they had insurance?
- Did you file a police report?
- Do you know whether you have uninsured motorist coverage?

### Property damage only
Ask:
- Was anyone injured?
- Are you seeking only vehicle repair or property damage reimbursement?

## Vista Auto Justice fit note

Best-fit cases for this firm:
- Southern California rear-end or simple auto accidents with injury
- Spanish-speaking callers or bilingual intake needed
- Southern California Uber/Lyft injuries
- moderate commercial-vehicle accidents that are not catastrophic

## Referral note

If the case is a better fit for another network firm, do not share client information until consent is granted.

title: Vista Auto Justice case acceptance and referral criteria
text:
# Vista Auto Justice — Case Acceptance and Referral Criteria

## Accept criteria

Vista Auto Justice should generally accept or request attorney review when:

- Southern California rear-end or simple auto accidents with injury
- Spanish-speaking callers or bilingual intake needed
- Southern California Uber/Lyft injuries
- moderate commercial-vehicle accidents that are not catastrophic

Additional required signals:
- Injury is mentioned.
- The caller is in coverage or the case has statewide coverage fit.
- The facts do not require a better-matched specialist firm.
- Attorney capacity is available or urgent review is justified.

## Priority triggers

Escalate priority when any of the following are present:
- Minor or child involved.
- ER, hospital, surgery, fracture, brain/spine injury, or severe ongoing symptoms.
- Commercial vehicle or employer-owned vehicle.
- Insurance company is pressuring the caller.
- Multiple parties are blaming each other.
- Hit-and-run or uninsured/underinsured driver.
- Caller asks "Do I have a case?" or requests legal evaluation.

## Decline/resource triggers

Use a decline/resource path when:
- No one was injured.
- Property damage only.
- Caller only wants repair reimbursement.
- Accident occurred outside service area and no network firm applies.
- Caller refuses consent for referral and no other workflow is available.

## Refer-out criteria

- severe commercial or catastrophic injury -> Summit Commercial Auto
- complex uninsured, hit-and-run, or disputed liability -> Pacific Complex Collision
- Bay Area clear-liability case -> BayBridge Auto Injury
- Bay Area disputed liability -> Pacific Complex Collision
- no injury / property damage only -> Decline/resource path

## Attorney review threshold

Send to attorney review when:
- Case is a possible fit but missing facts prevent acceptance.
- Minor involved and injury is possible.
- Caller asks for case-strength evaluation.
- High-priority facts are present but the matching firm should confirm.
- Capacity is limited and attorney must approve intake.

## Retrieval examples

- Query: "Me chocaron en Los Angeles. Tengo dolor en la espalda. Necesito hablar con alguien en español." -> retrieve this firm's acceptance criteria.
- Query: "A UPS truck hit me and I had surgery." -> retrieve referral criteria and partner match.

title: Vista Auto Justice intake call script
text:
# Vista Auto Justice — Intake Call Script

## Opening

"I'm sorry that happened. I can collect the key details for attorney review. I am not a lawyer and cannot determine whether you have a case, but I can help get the right information to the right team."

## Core questions

1. "Where and when did the accident happen?"
2. "Was anyone injured or treated by urgent care, the ER, or a doctor?"
3. "Was a child or minor involved?"
4. "Was a commercial vehicle, Uber/Lyft, multiple cars, hit-and-run, or uninsured driver involved?"
5. "Has any insurance company contacted you?"
6. "What is the best phone number for the firm to follow up?"

## Follow-up questions based on retrieved context

### If commercial vehicle
"Do you know the company name on the vehicle?"

### If ER/hospital
"Are you still receiving medical treatment?"

### If minor involved
"Was the child injured or evaluated by a medical provider?"

### If insurance pressure
"Have they asked for a recorded statement or for you to sign anything?"

### If referral likely
"Based on what you shared, this may be a better fit for a trusted partner firm. Your information is confidential, and we will not share it without your permission."

## Closing for attorney review

"Thank you. I will prepare this for attorney review and mark the missing details so the team can follow up."

## Closing for referral consent

"Do you consent to us sharing your case summary and contact information with the recommended partner firm so they can review whether they may be able to help?"

title: Vista Auto Justice referral consent policy
text:
# Vista Auto Justice — Referral Consent and Confidentiality Policy

## Confidential information

The following information must be treated as confidential and should not be shared with a partner firm until client consent is granted:

- Caller name
- Phone number
- Email
- Accident location/date
- Injury description
- Medical treatment details
- Insurance details
- Transcript excerpts
- Photos, documents, police report information, or witness details

## Consent prompt

"Based on what you shared, this matter may be a better fit for one of our trusted partner firms, [Receiving Firm]. Your information is confidential, and we will not share your case summary or contact information without your permission. Do you consent to us sharing your intake details with [Receiving Firm] so they can review whether they may be able to help?"

## Consent outcomes

### If consent is granted
- Mark consent status: granted.
- Unlock referral packet.
- Send packet to receiving firm.
- Notify client that packet has been sent.
- Create referral tracking record.

### If consent is denied
- Mark consent status: denied.
- Keep packet locked.
- Do not share externally.
- Offer a general resource response or ask whether the client wants more information.

### If client wants more information
- Provide partner firm name, coverage, why it may be a fit, and what information would be shared.
- Ask again for consent before sharing.

## Audit fields

- Consent status
- Timestamp
- Receiving firm
- Information categories shared
- User approving referral
- Packet sent status
